name.thai: ตลาดน้ำคลองแห

official_name: Khlong Hae Floating Market (ตลาดน้ำคลองแห)
address: Opposite Wat Khlong Hae, near Big C Supercenter, Klong Hae, Hat Yai District, Songkhla

description.en: Southern Thailand's first and only floating market. Khlong Hae features eco-friendly food in coconut shells and bamboo tubes, boat rides, and cultural shows. Open weekends and public holidays only.
description.nl: De eerste en enige drijvende markt in Zuid-Thailand. Khlong Hae serveert milieuvriendelijk eten in kokosnootschalen en bamboerietjes, boottochten en culturele shows. Alleen open in het weekend en op feestdagen.

highlights: First and only floating market in Southern Thailand
Eco-friendly food in coconut shells & bamboo tubes
Cultural performances with staff in Thai retro costumes
Multicultural: blends Buddhist Thai and Muslim food traditions
Popular with Indonesian, Malaysian & Singaporean visitors

metaTitle.en: Hat Yai Floating Market: Southern Thailand's Only Floating Market (2026)
metaTitle.nl: Hat Yai Drijvende Markt: Enige Drijvende Markt in Zuid-Thailand (2026)

metaDescription.en: Khlong Hae Floating Market in Hat Yai — open Fri-Sun 13:00-21:00. Free entry, eco-friendly food in coconut shells, boat rides & cultural shows. Popular with Malaysian and Singaporean visitors.
metaDescription.nl: Khlong Hae Drijvende Markt in Hat Yai — open vrij-zon 13:00-21:00. Gratis entree, milieuvriendelijk eten in kokosnootschalen, boottochten & culturele shows. Populair bij Maleisische en Singaporese bezoekers.

enhanced_description: Khlong Hae Floating Market (ตลาดน้ำคลองแห) is the first and only floating market in Southern Thailand, located opposite Wat Khlong Hae near Big C Supercenter in Hat Yai. Open Friday to Sunday and public holidays from 13:00 to 21:00, this market stands out for its eco-friendly approach: food is served in coconut shells, bamboo tubes, and earthen pots instead of plastic containers. The market uniquely blends Buddhist Thai and Muslim food traditions, reflecting Hat Yai's multicultural character as a border city. Staff wear traditional Thai retro costumes, adding to the cultural atmosphere. Most visitors are international tourists from Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore, drawn by the market's reputation for authentic Southern Thai street food, boat rides along the canal, and live cultural performances.
detailed_history: Khlong Hae Floating Market was established in 2000 to revive the traditional floating market culture that had largely disappeared from Southern Thailand. Unlike the famous floating markets of central Thailand (such as Damnoen Saduak and Amphawa), Khlong Hae is the only one of its kind in the south. The market was created as a community initiative to promote local entrepreneurship and preserve Southern Thai culinary traditions. It quickly became a major draw for cross-border tourists from Malaysia and Singapore, given Hat Yai's position as a gateway border city. The market has since evolved into a cultural showcase blending the region's Buddhist Thai and Muslim Malay food traditions.
visitor_experience: Khlong Hae Floating Market is located opposite Wat Khlong Hae, near Big C Supercenter — easy to find by tuk-tuk or taxi from central Hat Yai (about 20 minutes). The market layout features boats lined up along the canal selling food in eco-friendly coconut shells, bamboo tubes, and earthen pots. Staff in Thai retro costumes add charm to the experience. Expect a mix of Buddhist Thai and Muslim food options. Cultural shows and boat rides are available throughout the afternoon and evening. Most visitors are from Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore. Plan 1.5 to 2 hours for a comfortable visit.

best_time_to_visit.time_of_day: Afternoon to evening. The market opens at 13:00 (1 PM) and runs until 21:00 (9 PM). Arrive by 15:00-16:00 to explore in daylight, then stay for the atmospheric evening lighting.
best_time_to_visit.days_open: Friday, Saturday, Sunday, and Thai public holidays ONLY. Closed Monday through Thursday.
best_time_to_visit.duration: Recommended visit duration is 1.5 to 2 hours to fully enjoy the market's offerings.
best_time_to_visit.crowd_level: Busiest on Saturday evenings and public holidays. Friday afternoon is typically the quietest time to visit.

photography_tips: Best photo spots include the colorful boats filled with food and the lively performance stage.
Lighting conditions are best during the golden hour just before sunset and after dark when the market is beautifully lit.
Instagram-worthy locations include the floating food stalls and the vibrant cultural performances.
Be mindful of vendors' privacy and avoid photographing them without permission.

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Visitors can take a tuk-tuk, taxi, or local bus from Hat Yai city to Klong Hae; it's about a 20-minute ride.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Nearby attractions include the Hat Yai Municipal Park, Wat Hat Yai Nai, and the Magic Eye 3D Museum.
practical_info.food_options: Dining options within the market include a variety of local dishes such as pad thai, tom yum, and fresh seafood.
practical_info.shopping: Visitors can find souvenir shops selling local handicrafts and products in the area surrounding the market.

insider_tips: The market is only open Friday-Sunday and public holidays. Do NOT visit on weekdays — it will be closed.
Arrive around 13:00-14:00 when the market opens for the freshest food and smallest crowds.
Try the food served in coconut shells and bamboo tubes — it's the market's signature eco-friendly presentation.
Halal food is widely available due to the market's blend of Buddhist Thai and Muslim food traditions.
Don't miss the cultural performances and staff in Thai retro costumes — they're a highlight.
Most visitors are from Malaysia, Singapore, and Indonesia — expect a multilingual atmosphere.
seasonal_considerations: The market operates on weekends (Friday-Sunday) and public holidays year-round. During the rainy season (May to October), some stalls may close if weather is severe. The dry season (November to April) offers the most pleasant experience. Note: the market is closed Monday through Thursday regardless of season.
accessibility: The market may present challenges for visitors with mobility issues due to its layout and the presence of steps. It's advisable to check for accessible routes beforehand.
cultural_significance: Khlong Hae Floating Market holds special significance as the first and only floating market in Southern Thailand. It uniquely blends Buddhist Thai and Muslim food traditions, reflecting the multicultural identity of Hat Yai and Songkhla province. The market's eco-friendly practices — serving food in coconut shells, bamboo tubes, and earthen pots instead of plastic — preserve traditional Southern Thai food presentation methods. It serves as a vital bridge between Thai and Malay-Muslim culinary cultures and is a key driver of cross-border tourism from Malaysia, Singapore, and Indonesia.
fun_facts: Khlong Hae is the first and only floating market in all of Southern Thailand.
All food is served in coconut shells, bamboo tubes, and earthen pots — no plastic containers allowed.
Staff wear traditional Thai retro costumes to maintain the market's cultural atmosphere.
The majority of visitors are international tourists from Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore rather than domestic Thai travelers.
The market blends Buddhist Thai and Muslim food traditions, with halal options widely available.
